actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Theories are made of smaller building blocks of concepts that together form the main structure.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: Like a building, a theory is not sound if it lacks a strong foundation.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: The creator of a theory is like the architect of a building; both are designers who start with an inspiration.

actions.name: text-insert
actions.text: You need a theory to stand up to the evidence.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: You need a theory to stand up to the evidence just as you need a building to stand up to any physical forces that threaten its integrity.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A theory answers a question just as a building answers a challenge.

actions.name: text-insert
actions.text: If you believe in them, they'll keep you safe.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A building must be strong enough to withstand a hurricane just as a theory must be strong enough not to have holes poked in it by skeptics.

actions.name: text-insert
actions.text: If we build it, they will come.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A theory provides a framework for others to modify or build on, just as a building provides a structure for further renovations.

actions.name: text-insert
actions.text: You can't build a theory without taking the time to understand the world around you.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: You can't build a theory without taking the time to understand the world around you just as you can't construct a building without understanding the site.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A theory will crumble if one of its founding principles is flawed, just as a building will collapse if any part of its foundation is structurally weak.

actions.name: text-delete
actions.text: A building is only as strong as its design, just as a theory is only as strong as the idea behind it.
